entities.name: ENVIRONMENTAL CUES
entities.description: External signals that trigger secondary stress conditions in plants

entities.name: OSMOTIC STRESS
entities.description: A stress condition resulting from environmental cues that reduces crop yields

entities.name: OXIDATIVE STRESS
entities.description: A stress condition resulting from environmental cues that reduces crop yields

entities.name: CROP YIELDS
entities.description: The agricultural productivity of crops, which is reduced by osmotic and oxidative stress

entities.name: MOLECULAR OXYGEN
entities.description: O2, which is reduced in metabolic reactions to produce reactive oxygen species

entities.name: SUPEROXIDE ANIONS
entities.description: O2•−, a reactive oxygen species produced from the reduction of molecular oxygen

entities.name: REACTIVE OXYGEN SPECIES
entities.description: ROS, chemically reactive molecules that disturb redox potential and threaten cellular organelles

entities.name: REDOX POTENTIAL
entities.description: The cellular balance of reduction and oxidation disturbed by ROS

entities.name: CELLULAR ORGANELLES
entities.description: Internal cell structures threatened by the production of ROS

entities.name: HIGH LIGHT INTENSITY
entities.description: An environmental factor that increases the production of ROS

entities.name: PLANTS
entities.description: Living organisms that evolve strategies to minimize ROS accumulation

entities.name: AVOIDANCE MECHANISMS
entities.description: Strategies evolved by plants to minimize excess ROS accumulation

entities.name: PHYSIOLOGICAL ADAPTATION
entities.description: A type of avoidance mechanism used by plants

entities.name: PHOTOSYSTEMS
entities.description: Efficient systems such as C4 or CAM metabolism used to avoid ROS

entities.name: C4 METABOLISM
entities.description: An efficient photosynthetic system used as an avoidance mechanism

entities.name: CAM METABOLISM
entities.description: Crassulacean Acid Metabolism, an efficient photosynthetic system used as an avoidance mechanism

entities.name: SCAVENGING MECHANISMS
entities.description: Strategies involving antioxidants and enzymes to minimize ROS accumulation

entities.name: ANTIOXIDANTS
entities.description: Molecules produced as part of scavenging mechanisms to detoxify ROS

entities.name: ANTIOXIDATIVE ENZYMES
entities.description: Enzymes produced as part of scavenging mechanisms to detoxify ROS

entities.name: ASCORBATE-GLUTATHIONE PATHWAY
entities.description: A biochemical pathway critical for detoxifying excess ROS in plant cells

entities.name: SUPEROXIDE DISMUTASE
entities.description: SOD, an enzyme in the ascorbate-glutathione pathway that detoxifies superoxide anions

entities.name: ASCORBATE PEROXIDASE
entities.description: APX, an enzyme in the ascorbate-glutathione pathway that detoxifies hydrogen peroxide

entities.name: HYDROGEN PEROXIDE
entities.description: H2O2, a reactive oxygen species detoxified by ascorbate peroxidase

entities.name: MONODEHYDROASCORBATE REDUCTASE
entities.description: MDHAR, an enzyme involved in recycling ascorbate

entities.name: DEHYDROASCORBATE REDUCTASE
entities.description: DHAR, an enzyme involved in recycling ascorbate

entities.name: GLUTATHIONE REDUCTASE
entities.description: GR, an enzyme involved in recycling glutathione

entities.name: ASCORBATE
entities.description: A reduced substrate recycled by MDHAR and DHAR

entities.name: GLUTATHIONE
entities.description: A reduced substrate recycled by GR

entities.name: ABIOTIC STRESS
entities.description: Non-living environmental stress conditions that generate ROS

entities.name: GENOMICS
entities.description: An approach used to overcome oxidative stress

entities.name: GENETIC ENGINEERING
entities.description: An approach used to overcome oxidative stress
